author: parth1595
text: Converting onnx model to tensorflow model using Saver method fails

author: parth1595
text:
I have already converted the onnx model to tensorflow model using SaveModelBuilder method and it works fine. But I want the final pb to be which should be generated using .meta .data and .index file and should be directly be able to load in tensorboard utility using **import\_pb\_to\_tensorboard.py**

Here is the snippet code which I have tried to generate tensorflow model using Saver method:

```
onnx.checker.check_model(onnx_model)
print('prepare tf model')
tf_rep = prepare(onnx_model)

if path.exists(export_path):
    shutil.rmtree(export_path)
with tf.Session() as sess:
    print("load graph")
    sess.graph.as_default()
    tf.import_graph_def(tf_rep.graph.as_graph_def(), name='')

    saver = tf.train.Saver()
    saver.save(sess, ".")
    tf.train.write_graph(sess.graph.as_graph_def(), "./", "parth", true)
```

**Error:**

Traceback (most recent call last):  
File "converter\_modify\_1.py", line 42, in   
saver = tf.train.Saver()  
File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/tensorflow/python/training/saver.py", line 1284, in **init**  
self.build()  
File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/tensorflow/python/training/saver.py", line 1296, in build  
self.\_build(self.\_filename, build\_save=True, build\_restore=True)  
File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/tensorflow/python/training/saver.py", line 1321, in \_build  
raise ValueError("No variables to save")  
ValueError: No variables to save

I have attached the onnx model and this is the mnist model.  
[model.onnx.zip](https://github.com/onnx/onnx-tensorflow/files/2404792/model.onnx.zip)

author: parth1595
text: [@tjingrant](https://github.com/tjingrant) I think the export\_graph method will only generate meta data.

author: fumihwh
text: [@parth1595](https://github.com/parth1595) I think you got `ValueError: No variables to save` because you need to init variable before call `saver = tf.train.Saver()`.

author: parth1595
text:
[@fumihwh](https://github.com/fumihwh) Yes I know but what i think there are no variables after we perform **tf\_rep = prepare(onnx\_model)**. But I am not sure of this if you know how to do this then please help me to solve this. Also I have attached the model in this thread so you can try it if you want to.  
Thanks

author: fumihwh
text:
[@parth1595](https://github.com/parth1595) I do **import\_to\_tensorboard** successfully.

```
import onnx
from tensorflow.python.tools.import_pb_to_tensorboard import import_to_tensorboard

from onnx_tf.backend import prepare

onnx_model = onnx.load("pb/model_var.onnx")

tf_rep = prepare(onnx_model)
tf_rep.export_graph("tb_pb/model_var.pb")

import_to_tensorboard("tb_pb/model_var.pb", "tb_log")
```

Then `tensorboard --logdir=tb_log`

author: chenyuZha
text:
[@fumihwh](https://github.com/fumihwh) I have export the model onnx to model tensorflow (.pb), but when I tried to run the inference by calling this model tensorflow (.pb), I had the problem between **NHWC** and **NCHW.** As you see, the onnx model is arranged like NCHW but tensorflow model is NHWC..  
So when I load my image by opencv to feed my input\_tensor, I have this problem:  
`ValueError: Cannot feed value of shape (512, 1024, 3) for Tensor '0:0', which has shape '(1, 3, 512, 1024)'`  
So I reload my input image by permute the channels, then another error occurs:  
`2018-09-24 16:50:10.159484: E tensorflow/core/common_runtime/executor.cc:645] Executor failed to create kernel. Invalid argument: Conv2DCustomBackpropInputOp only supports NHWC.`   
Any suggestions to solve this issue?

text: [@chenyuZha](https://github.com/chenyuZha) Did you solve this? I get somewhat the same issue when exporting my model to tensorflow serve, more info here:[https://stackoverflow.com/questions/55192452/tf-saved-model-acting-different-when-run-at-endpoint](url)
